title: Meal Plan auto-sync to Shopping List (Mealie)
feature_summary: Add an automatic Meal Plan → Shopping List sync feature to Mealie. Every household can opt in via three new user-configurable HouseholdPreferences fields (auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping, auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id, auto_sync_run_time) plus one new user-configurable timezone field and one server-only last_auto_synced_at infrastructure column. A new scheduler task auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping registers on the existing register_minutely (5-min) bucket — Mealie has no native 30-min bucket and input §'实现约束' forbids creating one — and gates per-household to a 30-min window so the effective per-household execution is once per household-local day. The task fetches today's MealPlan entries via RepositoryMeals.get_today(tz=...), re-fetches the full Recipe per entry (since ReadPlanEntry.recipe is RecipeSummary and has no recipe_ingredient), filters out ingredients whose Food.is_pantry_staple = True (a NEW column on IngredientFoodModel), and feeds the filtered ingredients into the existing consolidation seam — ShoppingListService.add_recipe_ingredients_to_list (which internally invokes get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe → bulk_create_items → can_merge + merge_items). The target list defaults to the oldest household shopping list (created_at asc) when auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id is None. A new EventTypes.meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping event is dispatched. A household-admin POST /api/households/preferences/auto-sync-shopping/run-now endpoint shares the per-household helper with the scheduler, bypasses the daily limit (still updating last_auto_synced_at per the matrix in §6), and returns AutoSyncRunResult { added_count, skipped_pantry_count, target_list_id, run_at }. Multi-replica safety via a single CAS UPDATE on last_auto_synced_at. Strict per-household tenant isolation enforced by the household-scoped repositories.

naming_reconciliations.decision: Extend UpdateHouseholdPreferences and reuse the existing PUT; no new PATCH route. See FR-10.

naming_reconciliations.mealie_reality: No such top-level function exists (ripgrep confirms 0 matches in mealie/). Consolidation lives inside ShoppingListService.bulk_create_items (shopping_lists.py:154-223).
naming_reconciliations.decision: Reuse ShoppingListService.add_recipe_ingredients_to_list as the canonical seam. If case-3 ships an extracted consolidate_ingredients, case-5 must call it directly. See FR-17 + SCN-1.

naming_reconciliations.mealie_reality: scheduler_service.py:15-17 defines only MINUTES_5, MINUTES_HOUR, MINUTES_DAY. Input §'实现约束' forbids creating new buckets.
naming_reconciliations.decision: Register on register_minutely (5-min) and gate the task to a 30-min window per household. Effective per-household execution = once per household-local day. See FR-13/FR-14/FR-16 + SC-1.
Use the existing convention. See FR-26.

user_stories.story: As a household admin, I want to turn on auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping and configure auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id, auto_sync_run_time, and timezone via PUT /api/households/preferences, so that the system knows my household opted in and when/where to sync.

As a household member, I want the scheduler to run automatically at my configured auto_sync_run_time in my household's timezone and append today's meal-plan recipes to my chosen shopping list, so that my shopping list is always up-to-date without manual intervention.
As a household admin, I want ingredients whose food is flagged is_pantry_staple = True to be excluded from the auto-sync, so that staples (flour, salt, oil) don't clutter my shopping list.

As a household member, I want the scheduler to sync at most once per household-local day, even if the scheduler ticks multiple times within the 30-min window or runs on multiple worker replicas, so that my list isn't duplicated.

As a household admin, I want to trigger an immediate sync via POST /api/households/preferences/auto-sync-shopping/run-now that bypasses the daily limit, so that I can re-sync after editing today's meal plan without waiting for the next scheduled run.

As a notifier/webhook subscriber, I want the system to dispatch a meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping event with {household_id, shopping_list_id, added_item_count, skipped_pantry_count} after each successful auto-sync, so that downstream integrations can react.

As a user in household A (group G1) and a user in household B (group G1 or G2), I want household A's auto-sync to never read household B's meal plan or write to household B's shopping list, so that tenant boundaries hold even when the scheduler iterates all households in a single process.

functional_requirements.requirement: Add 5 columns to HouseholdPreferencesModel: (a) auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping: Mapped[bool] (sa.Boolean, non-null, default False); (b) auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id: Mapped[GUID | None] (FK to shopping_lists.id, ondelete='SET NULL', nullable); (c) auto_sync_run_time: Mapped[str] (sa.String(5), non-null, default '00:00'); (d) last_auto_synced_at: Mapped[datetime | None] (NaiveDateTime, nullable — server-only, written by CAS UPDATE only); (e) timezone: Mapped[str | None] (sa.String, nullable — IANA name). Use FilterableColumn typing consistent with surrounding fields. The @auto_init() decorator at L42 picks up user-settable fields automatically.

Provide alembic migration <ts>_add_auto_sync_to_household_preferences.py that (a) op.add_column(...) for each of the 5 columns nullable=True, (b) populates defaults via UPDATE household_preferences SET auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping = FALSE/0, auto_sync_run_time = '00:00' WHERE id IS NOT NULL (postgres vs sqlite branch on the bool literal), (c) batch_alter_table('household_preferences') → batch_op.alter_column('auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping', nullable=False) and same for auto_sync_run_time, (d) downgrade drops the 5 columns in reverse declaration order. Chain: down_revision = '<rev of FR-4 migration>' (i.e., this migration runs after the add_is_pantry_staple_to_ingredient_foods migration).

Add is_pantry_staple: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, nullable=False, default=False) to IngredientFoodModel. Place after the name_normalized/plural_name_normalized block (after L183). Do NOT remove the deprecated on_hand (L192) — leave it intact for backward compatibility. Scope decision: per-Food (group-shared); see NC-3 below.

Provide alembic migration <ts>_add_is_pantry_staple_to_ingredient_foods.py mirroring add_staple_flag_to_foods exactly: batch_alter_table('ingredient_foods') → batch_op.add_column('is_pantry_staple', Boolean, nullable=True, default=False) → branch on postgres/sqlite for UPDATE ingredient_foods SET is_pantry_staple = FALSE/0 → batch_op.alter_column('is_pantry_staple', nullable=False). Downgrade drops the column. Chain: down_revision = '2187537c52b8' (current head).

Add Boolean subscriber column meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping: Mapped[bool] = mapped_column(Boolean, default=False, nullable=False) to GroupEventNotifierOptionsModel. Place after shopping_list_deleted (L37).

Provide alembic migration <ts>_add_meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping_event.py mirroring the existing precedent: batch_alter_table('group_events_notifier_options', schema=None) → batch_op.add_column('meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping', Boolean, nullable=False, default=False, server_default=sa.sql.expression.false()). Downgrade drops the column. Chain: down_revision = '<rev of FR-2 migration>'.

Extend UpdateHouseholdPreferences with 4 user-settable fields: auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping: bool = False, auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id: UUID4 | None = None, auto_sync_run_time: str = '00:00', timezone: str | None = None. Do NOT include last_auto_synced_at — it is server-only (see SCN-3). Add @field_validator('auto_sync_run_time') enforcing regex ^([01]\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\d$. Add @field_validator('timezone') calling ZoneInfo(v) and returning v (raising ValueError on invalid). Inheritance cascades to CreateHouseholdPreferences, SaveHouseholdPreferences, ReadHouseholdPreferences. Additionally, add last_auto_synced_at: datetime | None = None only to ReadHouseholdPreferences (so the UI can display it; never accept it as input).
Extend CreateIngredientFood with is_pantry_staple: bool = False. Field automatically cascades to SaveIngredientFood (extends CreateIngredientFood) and IngredientFood (extends CreateIngredientFood).

New Pydantic model AutoSyncRunResult(MealieModel) with fields added_count: int, skipped_pantry_count: int, target_list_id: UUID4 | None, run_at: datetime. Place in a new module mealie/schema/household/auto_sync.py; export via mealie/schema/household/__init__.py (auto-generated by task dev:generate).

The existing PUT /api/households/preferences endpoint must accept the 4 new user-settable fields via the extended UpdateHouseholdPreferences body, with no route-signature change. Before calling repos.household_preferences.update(...), if new_pref.auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id is not None: look it up via self.repos.group_shopping_lists.get_one(new_pref.auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id); if result is None or result.household_id != self.household_id, raise HTTPException(status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail='Shopping list does not belong to this household'). The existing self.checks.can_manage_household() gate (L60) remains. last_auto_synced_at is not in the UpdateHouseholdPreferences body (per FR-7), so no exclusion logic is needed at the route layer.

Add new endpoint POST /api/households/preferences/auto-sync-shopping/run-now on HouseholdSelfServiceController returning AutoSyncRunResult with status_code=200. Body: none. Gate: self.checks.can_manage_household(). Implementation calls the shared helper _sync_one_household(session=self.repos.session, group_id=self.group_id, household_id=self.household_id, bypass_enabled_check=True, bypass_daily_limit=True) (the bypass flags exist for the manual path; see FR-13/FR-20 ordering). Synchronous response (no BackgroundTasks for the sync work). Event dispatch happens inside the helper.

The existing PUT /api/foods/{item_id} endpoint at IngredientFoodsController.update_one must accept the new is_pantry_staple field via the extended CreateIngredientFood body, with no route-signature change. Existing self.checks.can_organize() gate (L71) is the permission boundary. Note: can_organize is Mealie's canonical food-management permission (household-level, not the global admin flag); input §4's 'admin' wording maps here.

Create new file mealie/services/scheduler/tasks/auto_sync_shopping.py exporting two callables: (a) auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping() -> None — top-level entry function, no args; (b) _sync_one_household(session, group_id, household_id, *, bypass_enabled_check=False, bypass_daily_limit=False) -> dict — internal helper shared with the run-now route. The entry function opens session_context(), iterates groups via repos.groups.page_all(PaginationQuery(page=1, per_page=-1)), then per-group constructs group_repos = get_repositories(session, group_id=group.id) and iterates households via group_repos.households.page_all(...). Per household it calls _sync_one_household(session, group.id, household.id) wrapped in try/except Exception: session.rollback(); logger.exception(...); continue. Tenant isolation: every read/write inside the helper uses get_repositories(session, group_id=group_id, household_id=household_id) (or the explicit household_id=None group-scope when reading recipes per FR-17), and the per-household block constructs its own scoped repos — never reuses the entry function's unscoped repos.

Register auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping in SchedulerRegistry.register_minutely(...) at mealie/app.py:134-136 — append to the existing positional args. Per input §'实现约束' forbidding new parallel schedulers, this reuses the existing 5-min bucket (scheduler_service.py:16 MINUTES_5 = 5 + L77-81 run_minutely). The 30-min effective interval is achieved by per-household window gating inside the task (FR-16), not by scheduler-bucket changes.
Add from .auto_sync_shopping import auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping to mealie/services/scheduler/tasks/__init__.py and append 'auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping' to __all__.

Inside _sync_one_household, resolve the timezone via _resolve_tz(prefs.timezone) — a helper that returns ZoneInfo(prefs.timezone) if set + valid, else ZoneInfo('UTC') (catching ZoneInfoNotFoundError / ValueError). Compute now_local = datetime.now(tz=tz) and now_utc = datetime.now(UTC). Compute the 30-min window: parse prefs.auto_sync_run_time into (hour, minute); let run_dt = now_local.replace(hour=hour, minute=minute, second=0, microsecond=0); the gate is 0 <= (now_local - run_dt).total_seconds() < 30 * 60. Skip the household if outside the window (return zero result) unless bypass_daily_limit=True. Forbid any use of datetime.now() without tz=... (per input §'实现约束').

Re-fetch and filter pipeline (replaces v1's flawed mp.recipe.recipe_ingredient access). Construct a group-scoped (household=None) recipes repo exactly like the existing seam: group_recipes_repo = get_repositories(session, group_id=group_id, household_id=None).recipes. For each mp in repos.meals.get_today(tz=tz): skip if mp.recipe_id is None; call full_recipe = group_recipes_repo.get_one(mp.recipe_id, 'id'); skip if full_recipe is None or not full_recipe.recipe_ingredient (EC-4); filter full_recipe.recipe_ingredient keeping only ingredients where ing.food is None or not getattr(ing.food, 'is_pantry_staple', False); count skipped ones in skipped_pantry_count; if filtered list is non-empty, append ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk(recipe_id=mp.recipe_id, recipe_increment_quantity=1.0, recipe_ingredients=filtered_ingredients). Then call ShoppingListService(repos).add_recipe_ingredients_to_list(list_id=target_list_id, recipe_items=recipe_items) and compute added_count = len(item_changes.created_items) + len(item_changes.updated_items). Do NOT roll a custom merge/aggregate function; reuse this seam verbatim. No top-level consolidate_ingredients function exists in Mealie today — the consolidation core is inside bulk_create_items. If case-3 ships an extracted public consolidate_ingredients, case-5 must call it directly (see SCN-1).

Append strategy is the existing bulk_create_items behaviour: items with the same (food_id, unit_id) as an existing unchecked item in the target list are merged (quantity accumulated via merge_items → merge_quantity_and_unit); otherwise inserted as new rows. Checked items are never merged into. The can_merge check enforces unit-compatibility via UnitConverter.

recipe_references are populated by get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe at lines 377-384 (each ShoppingListItemCreate carries recipe_references=[ShoppingListItemRecipeRefCreate(recipe_id, recipe_quantity, recipe_scale, recipe_note)]). This is automatic via the seam in FR-17. The Mealie data model has no meal_plan_id field on ShoppingListItemRecipeReference (shopping_list.py:26-48) — the link from a list item back to a meal-plan entry is event-only (via the dispatched EventMealPlanAutoSyncedData). The recipe-level back-link is preserved as ShoppingListItemRecipeRefCreate(recipe_id=mp.recipe_id, ...). Case-5 must NOT bypass get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe.

Ordered pipeline inside _sync_one_household (ensures EC-1 / EC-2 retry semantics are deterministic): (1) Load prefs = repos.household_preferences.get_one(household_id, 'household_id'); if None → return zero result. (2) If not bypass_enabled_check and not prefs.auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping → return zero result. (3) Resolve tz, now_local, now_utc; if not bypass_daily_limit and not in window per FR-16 → return zero result. (4) Resolve target_list_id per FR-22; if still None → log WARNING with i18n auto-sync.no-target-list and return zero result (do NOT update last_auto_synced_at — household retries on next tick). (5) Fetch mealplans = repos.meals.get_today(tz=tz). (6) CAS gate (scheduler-only path): if not bypass_daily_limit, execute UPDATE household_preferences SET last_auto_synced_at = :now WHERE id = :id AND (last_auto_synced_at IS NULL OR last_auto_synced_at < :today_start) with :now = now_utc.replace(tzinfo=None), :id = str(prefs.id), :today_start = datetime.combine(now_local.date(), datetime.min.time(), tzinfo=tz).astimezone(UTC).replace(tzinfo=None). If result.rowcount == 0 → already synced today or another worker won → return zero result. Else session.commit() immediately. (7) If not mealplans → log WARNING with i18n auto-sync.no-meal-plan-today; scheduler path already updated marker in step 6 (so empty days don't re-trigger); manual path writes UPDATE household_preferences SET last_auto_synced_at = :now WHERE id = :id and commits per the matrix in §6; return AutoSyncRunResult(added_count=0, skipped_pantry_count=0, target_list_id=target_list_id, run_at=now_utc) without dispatching the event. (8) Run filter + add_recipe_ingredients_to_list per FR-17; if bypass_daily_limit and added_count + skipped_pantry > 0, write the manual-path marker UPDATE; dispatch event per FR-24; return result. Works on both SQLite and Postgres (no FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED).
Manual trigger path (bypass_daily_limit=True) skips the CAS gate in step 6. The marker update on the manual path follows the matrix in §6 — see 'Manual trigger: last_auto_synced_at update matrix'.

When prefs.auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id is None, pick the oldest household shopping list by calling repos.group_shopping_lists.page_all(PaginationQuery(page=1, per_page=1, order_by='created_at', order_direction='asc')). The repos variable is the household-scoped one constructed in FR-13 (get_repositories(session, group_id=group_id, household_id=household_id)); per repository_generic.py:94-102 _filter_builder, the page_all call automatically filters by household_id — so the candidate set is strictly this household's shopping lists. If items is empty, log WARNING with i18n key auto-sync.no-target-list and per FR-20 step 4 return early without writing last_auto_synced_at. The terms 'first active' and 'main' used in input/v1 are dropped (no such fields exist in case-5 scope).

Add new enum member meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping = auto() to EventTypes, placed after shopping_list_deleted (line 44). Add a new payload class EventMealPlanAutoSyncedData(EventDocumentDataBase) with document_type: EventDocumentType = EventDocumentType.shopping_list, operation: EventOperation = EventOperation.create, household_id: UUID4, shopping_list_id: UUID4, added_item_count: int, skipped_pantry_count: int. The base-class fields (document_type, operation) are standard event metadata and are not considered 'extra payload' per SC-4.

Dispatch the event inside _sync_one_household immediately after a successful add_recipe_ingredients_to_list call (FR-20 step 8): EventBusService(session=session).dispatch(integration_id=INTERNAL_INTEGRATION_ID, group_id=group_id, household_id=household_id, event_type=EventTypes.meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping, document_data=EventMealPlanAutoSyncedData(household_id=household_id, shopping_list_id=target_list_id, added_item_count=added_count, skipped_pantry_count=skipped_pantry)). Always pass an explicit household_id (never None) — per event_bus_service.py:82-96, a None household_id causes the dispatcher to fan out to all households in the group. The payload also carries household_id redundantly so subscribers receive a self-describing event.

Add a new top-level auto-sync namespace to mealie/lang/messages/en-US.json with three keys: no-meal-plan-today ('No meal plan entries for today; auto-sync skipped'), no-target-list ('No target shopping list configured and no shopping lists exist in this household'), already-synced-today ('This household has already been auto-synced today'). Modify only en-US.json per .github/copilot-instructions.md (other locales are Crowdin-managed and PRs touching them are rejected).

New unit test file tests/unit_tests/services_tests/scheduler/tasks/test_auto_sync.py (>=7 tests). Required coverage: (a) test_consolidate_by_food_and_unit — call bulk_create_items directly with two ShoppingListItemCreate of the same (food_id, unit_id) and assert one merged item with quantity = sum(quantities) (smoke for case-3 coupling); (b) test_pantry_staple_filter — recipe with 2 ingredients (one staple, one not), assert added_count == 1, skipped_pantry_count == 1; (c) test_window_gates_outside_run_time — set auto_sync_run_time 2 hours in the past, assert task is a no-op; (d) test_window_gates_to_once_per_day — call task function synchronously 10 times within the same household-local day, assert add_recipe_ingredients_to_list invoked exactly once via monkeypatch; (e) test_concurrent_two_worker_simulation — open two sessions, run CAS UPDATE from both, assert exactly one returns rowcount == 1; (f) test_no_target_list_does_not_update_marker — household has 0 shopping lists, assert last_auto_synced_at stays None after a tick (EC-2); (g) test_no_households_with_autosync_smoke — task runs with zero opt-ins, does not raise. Test pattern: import the bare task function and invoke synchronously, mirroring test_create_timeline_events.py:18; never await @repeat_every.

New integration test file tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_auto_sync_run_now.py (>=6 tests). Required coverage: (a) happy path POST .../run-now returns 200 + AutoSyncRunResult shape; (b) non-can_manage_household user returns 403; (c) auto-sync disabled preference makes scheduler skip but run-now still succeeds (because manual trigger bypasses enabled check per FR-11); (d) no meal plans today returns 200 with added_count=0; (e) pantry-staple excluded from result; (f) event dispatched with expected payload via monkeypatch.setattr(EventBusService, 'dispatch', spy).

New multitenant test file tests/multitenant_tests/test_auto_sync_isolation.py (>=4 tests). Required coverage: (a) test_autosync_isolation_cross_group — household A in G1 syncs; household B in G2 sees no new items (uses multitenants fixture); (b) test_autosync_isolation_cross_household_same_group — household A in G1 syncs; household H2 in same G1 sees no new items in its own shopping list (uses h2_user fixture); (c) test_pantry_staple_isolation_cross_group — flip is_pantry_staple on a food in G1; verify a food in G2 with the same name is unaffected (groups have separate IngredientFoodModel rows because group_id scopes foods); (d) test_pantry_staple_shared_cross_household_same_group_by_design — flip is_pantry_staple on a food in G1; verify H2 in same G1 observes the flag = True (this is the spec-literal per-Food semantic, NOT a bug; see NC-3 resolution).

manual_trigger_marker_matrix.fr20_step: step 2, but bypass_enabled_check=True skips this — proceeds
step 3, but bypass_daily_limit=True skips this — proceeds

manual_trigger_marker_matrix.outcome: Successful sync (>=1 ingredient written or skipped)
Exception during sync work (e.g., DB error)
Configured target list deleted between config and call (EC-3)
manual_trigger_marker_matrix.fr20_step: step 4 (since FK SET NULL makes resolution fall through to FR-22)
manual_trigger_marker_matrix.last_auto_synced_at_updated: No if EC-2 path; Yes if EC-1/sync path

success_criteria.criterion: After app startup, auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping is present in SchedulerRegistry._minutely (asserted by importing both and checking auto_sync_meal_plan_to_shopping in SchedulerRegistry._minutely). The scheduler tick interval remains 5 minutes (scheduler_service.py:16 MINUTES_5 = 5); the per-household window gate (FR-16) plus CAS guard (FR-20) reduce the effective per-household execution to once per household-local day. SC-2 + the unit test in FR-26(d) cover the once-per-day guarantee.

When the scheduler task is invoked 10 consecutive times within the same household-local day (with the same (group_id, household_id)), add_recipe_ingredients_to_list is called exactly once for that household (asserted via spy/monkeypatch in test_window_gates_to_once_per_day).

Given a recipe with 2 ingredients where one ingredient's food.is_pantry_staple = True and the other's is False, the auto-sync writes exactly one new shopping-list item (the non-staple one); AutoSyncRunResult.skipped_pantry_count == 1 and AutoSyncRunResult.added_count == 1 where added_count = len(item_changes.created_items) + len(item_changes.updated_items).

The dispatched EventMealPlanAutoSyncedData payload contains only household_id, shopping_list_id, added_item_count, skipped_pantry_count beyond the standard EventDocumentDataBase metadata (document_type, operation) — no recipe titles, no meal-plan IDs, no per-item details (asserted in test_event_dispatched). The dispatched household_id matches the one being synced (no fan-out per FR-24).

When household A in group G1 runs auto-sync, household B's shopping list — whether B is in G1 (different household, same group) or G2 (different group entirely) — has zero new items afterward (asserted via test_autosync_isolation_cross_group and test_autosync_isolation_cross_household_same_group).

When POST .../auto-sync-shopping/run-now is called twice consecutively within the same household-local day, both calls return 200 with non-zero added_count on the first call (assuming meal plans exist) and the idempotent merge result on the second call (bulk_create_items may produce 0 new items + >=0 merged updates), and last_auto_synced_at is updated to the second call's timestamp only if the call took the success path per the matrix in §6.

(a) When prefs.auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id is None and the household has >=1 shopping list, AutoSyncRunResult.target_list_id equals the oldest household-scoped shopping list's id (order_by='created_at' asc). (b) When the household has 0 shopping lists, target_list_id is None, all counts are zero, a WARNING with the i18n key auto-sync.no-target-list is logged, and last_auto_synced_at is not updated (so the household retries on the next tick — EC-2).

All three new alembic migrations have working downgrade() functions (op.drop_column(...) for each added column). Migrations apply cleanly on a fresh SQLite DB and on a fresh PostgreSQL DB (per task py:postgres smoke). Chain: (a) add_is_pantry_staple_to_ingredient_foods → down_revision = '2187537c52b8'; (b) add_auto_sync_to_household_preferences → down_revision = '<rev of (a)>'; (c) add_meal_plan_auto_synced_to_shopping_event → down_revision = '<rev of (b)>'.
Test count delta: >=7 unit + >=6 integration + >=4 multitenant = >=17 new tests total. task py:test passes with 0 failures (including all pre-existing tests).

Per-tick performance bound: the scheduler task completes within 2 seconds per household under nominal conditions (<=20 meal-plan entries per day, <=50 ingredients per recipe, target list with <=500 existing items). Asserted indirectly via pytest --durations=10 after the full test run.

Cross-database safety: the CAS UPDATE statement uses text(...) with bound parameters :now, :id, :today_start (identical to FR-20 wording); works on both SQLite and Postgres (no FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED); result.rowcount == 0 is the sole authority on whether to proceed. Verified by running tests/unit_tests/services_tests/scheduler/tasks/test_auto_sync.py against both backends via task py:test and task py:postgres.

TypeScript types regenerate cleanly: after task dev:generate, frontend/app/lib/api/types/household.ts contains the 4 new user-settable HouseholdPreferences fields and the read-only last_auto_synced_at, and AutoSyncRunResult is exported, with no manual edits.

edge_cases.scenario: Meal plan is empty for today (household has 0 GroupMealPlan entries dated today_local).
edge_cases.expected_behaviour: FR-20 step 7. Scheduler path: CAS UPDATE already fired at step 6 (marks day as synced to avoid repeated empty scans); add_recipe_ingredients_to_list is NOT called; event NOT dispatched. Manual path: writes last_auto_synced_at per §6 matrix; returns AutoSyncRunResult(added_count=0, skipped_pantry_count=0, target_list_id=<resolved>, run_at=now_utc). WARNING-level log with i18n key auto-sync.no-meal-plan-today.

edge_cases.scenario: auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id is None AND household has 0 shopping lists.
edge_cases.expected_behaviour: FR-20 step 4. Both paths return early with target_list_id=None, added_count=0, skipped_pantry_count=0. Scheduler logs WARNING with auto-sync.no-target-list and does NOT update last_auto_synced_at (so the household retries on the next valid tick — admin may create a list later). Manual returns 200 with the same shape and also does NOT update last_auto_synced_at. Event NOT dispatched.

edge_cases.scenario: auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id points to a list that was deleted between configuration time and scheduler tick.
edge_cases.expected_behaviour: With ondelete='SET NULL' on the FK (FR-1), the column is auto-nulled by the DB on the cascade delete. Behaviour then matches EC-2 fallback (or the oldest-list rule if one still exists). The scheduler does NOT attempt to write to the deleted id and does NOT 500.

edge_cases.scenario: Recipe R is referenced by a meal-plan entry but R was deleted (or its recipe_id no longer resolves) between meal-plan creation and sync run.
edge_cases.expected_behaviour: FR-17: full_recipe = group_recipes_repo.get_one(mp.recipe_id, 'id') returns None → entry is skipped silently. added_count and skipped_pantry_count do not include it. No exception.

edge_cases.scenario: Household timezone changes from America/New_York to Asia/Tokyo mid-day.
edge_cases.expected_behaviour: now_local is computed fresh on each tick from ZoneInfo(prefs.timezone). The today_start_utc derived from the new tz may move the daily boundary, possibly enabling a second sync in the same wall-clock day. Accepted (admin opted in to the change); the CAS guard still prevents double-runs within the new household-local day.

edge_cases.scenario: Food is flagged is_pantry_staple = True after today's sync has already added that food to the list.
edge_cases.expected_behaviour: No retroactive removal. The previously-added item remains in the shopping list with all its recipe_references. The flag only affects future sync runs (the next day's add_recipe_ingredients_to_list will skip it). No i18n / runtime message — this semantic is documented in release notes only (no FR carries a runtime help string for this).

edge_cases.scenario: last_auto_synced_at exists in the future (e.g., server clock drifted backward 5 minutes; the column was set 5 minutes ago in what was then 'now').
edge_cases.expected_behaviour: CAS predicate (last_auto_synced_at IS NULL OR last_auto_synced_at < :today_start) only compares against today's local-midnight UTC, not 'now'. A future last_auto_synced_at (within today) still skips the run, which is correct. A last_auto_synced_at from a previous local day (regardless of clock drift) allows the run.

edge_cases.scenario: A meal-plan recipe contains a sub-recipe (RecipeIngredient.referenced_recipe is not None), and one of the sub-recipe's ingredients has is_pantry_staple = True.
edge_cases.expected_behaviour: Intentional limitation: FR-17's pantry filter only inspects top-level recipe_ingredient entries on the meal-plan recipe. The sub-recipe ingredients are pulled in by get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe recursion (shopping_lists.py:344-355) after the filter ran, so pantry staples inside sub-recipes are not skipped. Documented as NC-4; deferred scope. The non-pantry sub-recipe ingredients still get the same (food_id, unit_id) consolidation.

needs_clarification.question: Should the manual trigger reset last_auto_synced_at on every successful run, or only when added_count > 0?
needs_clarification.decision_in_v2: Decided: see §6 matrix. Update on success when sync work was performed (steps 7/8 of FR-20); do NOT update on EC-2 / exceptions. Resolved as non-blocking.

needs_clarification.question: Behaviour when auto_sync_target_shopping_list_id points to a list that becomes archived (case-2 may add an 'archived' flag).
needs_clarification.decision_in_v2: Treat as valid for case-5 (the column doesn't exist today). If case-2 ships an archived flag, case-5 must be patched to honour it — but that's case-2's responsibility to coordinate via a follow-up PR.

needs_clarification.question: Scope of is_pantry_staple: per-Food (group-shared) or per-household via M2M (matching households_with_ingredient_food precedent at ingredient.py:160-162)?
needs_clarification.decision_in_v2: Decided: per-Food (group-shared), per input §4 literal wording ('Food 模型新增 `is_pantry_staple: bool`'). Input §5's '跨 household 不互相影响' is interpreted as cross-group isolation (which holds via group_id scoping on IngredientFoodModel). FR-28 enumerates both isolation contexts; cross-household same-group is shared by design (see FR-28(d)). If a future requirement re-scopes to per-household, redo via M2M and migrate from this column. Resolved as non-blocking.

needs_clarification.question: Should pantry-staple filtering recursively walk sub-recipes (referenced_recipe)?
needs_clarification.decision_in_v2: Decided: case-5 scope is top-level recipe ingredients only (EC-8). Recursive pantry filtering would require either a deep-walk in _sync_one_household or a parameter to get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe — both are out of scope for case-5. Deferred.

self_concerns.concern: Case-5 reuses ShoppingListService.add_recipe_ingredients_to_list which delegates to bulk_create_items for consolidation. If case-3 ships a refactor extracting a top-level consolidate_ingredients symbol, case-5 should be updated to call it directly. If case-3 introduces a regression to bulk_create_items, case-5 inherits it. No top-level consolidate_ingredients function exists in mealie/ today (ripgrep verified, 0 matches).
self_concerns.mitigation: (a) FR-26(a) test_consolidate_by_food_and_unit exercises the bulk_create_items seam directly — case-5 tests fail fast if case-3 breaks consolidation. (b) When case-3 lands an extracted symbol, the FR-17 call site is updated in a follow-up PR; the spec is explicit that case-5 must NOT roll its own.

self_concerns.concern: The new timezone field uses zoneinfo.ZoneInfo. Python stdlib since 3.9, Mealie runs 3.12 — no new dependency. However, ZoneInfo raises ZoneInfoNotFoundError if OS tzdata is missing (some minimal Linux/Windows containers).
self_concerns.mitigation: (a) _resolve_tz helper catches ZoneInfoNotFoundError and falls back to ZoneInfo('UTC'). (b) Pydantic @field_validator('timezone') (FR-7) catches invalid strings at write time. (c) Add tzdata to pyproject.toml dependencies only if CI confirms ZoneInfoNotFoundError in the test container; otherwise rely on stdlib.

self_concerns.concern: last_auto_synced_at is a single column on HouseholdPreferencesModel. If both the CAS UPDATE and a regular ORM update() from the PUT route fire concurrently, the ORM unit-of-work could overwrite the CAS marker. Mitigation enforced by FR-7: last_auto_synced_at is removed from UpdateHouseholdPreferences; it appears only on ReadHouseholdPreferences (read-only) and is written exclusively by raw session.execute(text(...)) inside _sync_one_household (FR-20). This guarantees the ORM never writes it via the PUT path.
self_concerns.mitigation: FR-7 + FR-10 audit: last_auto_synced_at is server-only; the route never sees it in the input body.

constraints: MUST reuse mealie/services/scheduler/ existing abstractions (no parallel scheduler). Concretely: register via SchedulerRegistry.register_minutely (the only 5-min bucket Mealie has); per-household window gating inside the task achieves the 30-min effective interval.

MUST reuse mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py existing merge/add-item functions. Specifically: ShoppingListService.add_recipe_ingredients_to_list. No top-level consolidate_ingredients function exists in Mealie today.
MUST reuse mealie/services/event_bus_service/ for event dispatch via EventBusService.dispatch(...).

Multi-replica deployment: use DB row-level lock (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED) OR last_auto_synced_at CAS to guarantee one worker per household per day. Selected: CAS (SQLite-compatible — FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED is Postgres-only).
Timezone: MUST use household-configured timezone (fallback directly to server default UTC; no group default exists in Mealie); forbidden to use datetime.now() without tz= argument.

All code must follow Mealie repo conventions per .github/copilot-instructions.md: Repository-Service-Controller pattern; routes delegate to services; services coordinate repos. Pydantic schema suffixes *In/*Out/*Create/*Update. DI via Depends(get_repositories), Depends(get_current_user). Type hints mandatory; mypy-compatible. Run task dev:generate after Pydantic schema changes (regenerates TS types + __init__.py exports). Only en-US locale modified for i18n. Migrations tested against SQLite + PostgreSQL. Use uv run ... for Python commands.
